meta.title: The Cogwright's Apprentice
meta.learner_goal_summary: User has prior NN exposure and wants to reinforce forward-pass and backprop math by manually implementing a small feedforward net from scratch in numpy, via a code-along story format.
assumptions_made: No specific dataset requested; a tiny synthetic XOR-style or linear-separable toy set is used so the math stays visible and the net is small (2-2-1).
User is comfortable with numpy arrays and broadcasting; no need to teach array basics.
'Concise and approachable' interpreted as 3 parts, ~30 min each, with a clear story wrapper but minimal fluff.

narrative_brief.theme: A clockwork apprentice building a 'thinking engine' from brass neurons for a quirky inventor
narrative_brief.setting: The workshop of Cogwright Aurelia, where neural nets are physical brass gates and levers rather than software â€” until the apprentice (learner) is told to build one in code
narrative_brief.premise: Aurelia needs a tiny brass brain that can learn a simple trick (like telling two lever patterns apart). The learner is her apprentice, handed the schematic and told to wire the forward flow, then teach it to learn by turning the correction cranks (backprop), all in Python.
narrative_brief.tone: Warm, lightly steampunk, code-along mentor voice â€” encourages tinkering, never condescending
narrative_brief.protagonist_or_frame_device: The learner is 'the Apprentice' receiving tasks from Cogwright Aurelia
narrative_brief.opening_hook_guidance: Aurelia hands the apprentice a 2-2-1 brass schematic and says 'wire the signal path first, we'll teach it later.' Under 120 words before Part 1.
narrative_brief.closing_note_guidance: Aurelia checks the ledger: the tiny net now separates the patterns. Frame as the apprentice's success log, not a recap lecture.

learning_objectives.objective: Implement a forward pass through a small FFN using matrix ops and sigmoid
Derive and implement backprop gradients for one hidden layer manually
Train via gradient descent and observe loss drop on a toy task

provided_classes_or_functions.name: sigmoid
provided_classes_or_functions.role: Fully implemented activation; keeps learner focused on pass/backprop structure
provided_classes_or_functions.notes_for_code_agent: def sigmoid(x): return 1/(1+np.exp(-x)); include stable note in docstring

environment_notes: Standard CPython 3 with numpy; no GPU mentioned

parts.title: Wiring the Signal Levers
parts.narrative_tie_in: Aurelia: 'Route the lever signals through the brass neurons. No learning yet â€” just show me the output for given weights.'
parts.learner_task_spec: Implement class TinyNet with __init__(self, w1, b1, w2, b2) storing weights as numpy arrays (shapes: w1 (2,2), b1 (2,), w2 (2,1), b2 (1,)). Implement forward(self, x) where x is (2,) or (N,2): return sigmoid(hidden) and sigmoid(output) via matrix ops. Return (out_hidden, out_final) tuple.

entities.name: TinyNet
entities.docstring_requirements: Describe layer shapes and forward contract

entities.name: forward
entities.docstring_requirements: Note expected input shape and returned tuple

edge_cases_to_handle: single sample (2,) vs batch (N,2) handling via np.atleast_2d or reshape
sigmoid overflow on large pre-activations (use provided stable sigmoid)

hints_tiered.tier_1_nudge: Hidden pre-activation is just x @ w1 + b1. What shape does b1 broadcast to?
hints_tiered.tier_2_partial: Use np.atleast_2d(x) at start of forward; compute h = sigmoid(np.dot(x2d, w1) + b1).
hints_tiered.tier_3_near_solution: out_hidden = sigmoid(x2d@w1 + b1); out_final = sigmoid(out_hidden@w2 + b2); return (out_hidden, out_final)

sample_test_cases.description: forward on known tiny weights matches hand calc

common_mistakes_to_flag: treating x as (2,) and getting shape errors

parts.title: Turning the Correction Cranks
parts.narrative_tie_in: Aurelia: 'It guessed wrong. Here's the error lever â€” now turn the cranks backward to find each weight's blame.'
parts.learner_task_spec: Add method backward(self, x, y) to TinyNet that computes gradients of MSE loss (mean over batch of (out_final - y)^2) w.r.t w1,b1,w2,b2 using chain rule. Return dict with keys 'dw1','db1','dw2','db2' as numpy arrays matching weight shapes. Reuse forward internally.

entities.name: backward
entities.docstring_requirements: State loss is MSE, return grad dict

edge_cases_to_handle: y shape (N,) vs (N,1) â€” reshape consistent
derivative of sigmoid: s*(1-s) applied to pre-activations

hints_tiered.tier_1_nudge: dLoss/dout_final = 2*(out_final-y)/N. Then push through sigmoid' and hidden.
hints_tiered.tier_2_partial: delta2 = (out_final-y) * out_final*(1-out_final) / N; dw2 = hidden.T @ delta2.
hints_tiered.tier_3_near_solution: delta1 = (delta2 @ w2.T) * h*(1-h); dw1 = x2d.T @ delta1; db = sum axis=0.

sample_test_cases.description: gradient check via finite diff on one weight
shapes of grads equal weight shapes

common_mistakes_to_flag: sigmoid derivative applied to post-activation not pre

parts.title: The Morning Ledger of Errors
parts.narrative_tie_in: Aurelia: 'Run the cranks 500 times on this lever pattern. Watch the ledger error shrink.'
parts.learner_task_spec: Implement train(net, X, Y, lr=0.1, epochs=500) that loops: forward, backward, update weights via w -= lr*grad, and returns list of loss per epoch. Use provided toy X (4x2 XOR-ish), Y (4x1). Print/return final loss.

entities.name: train
entities.signature: train(net, X, Y, lr=0.1, epochs=500) -> list

edge_cases_to_handle: loss should decrease monotonically-ish; if explode, lr note
X,Y as provided constants in scaffold or main

hints_tiered.tier_1_nudge: For each epoch: out = net.forward(X); grads = net.backward(X,Y); subtract lr*grad from each param.
hints_tiered.tier_2_partial: loss = np.mean((out[-1]-Y)**2); append to list before update.

sample_test_cases.description: loss after 500 epochs < 0.1 on toy XOR

grading_rubric.criterion: function reusable for any TinyNet instance

common_mistakes_to_flag: hardcoding X inside train instead of param

global_verification_strategy.autograder_approach: Deterministic with np.random.seed in tests for weight init; part 2 uses finite-diff gradient check; part 3 asserts loss threshold.
global_verification_strategy.test_harness_notes: Seed numpy before constructing TinyNet in tests; tolerance 1e-4 for grad checks.
manual_review_needed_for: clarity of backprop math comments in code (rubric read)

optional_extensions.title: Different Activation
optional_extensions.description: Swap sigmoid for tanh in a copy of TinyNet and observe training speed change on same toy set

optional_extensions.title: Deeper Brass
optional_extensions.description: Add a third layer to TinyNet and extend backward manually

style_guide_for_downstream_agents.tone: Apprentice-mentor steampunk, code-along, precise under flavor
style_guide_for_downstream_agents.code_style: PEP8, numpy snake_case, PascalCase class, docstrings on public methods
consistency_rules: Class TinyNet, methods forward/backward, function train, provided sigmoid must be verbatim across agents.
Use 'lever' for input, 'crank' for gradient step, 'ledger' for loss in all text.
No PyTorch/TensorFlow references in any agent output.
